economic_notes: Band operates Economic Development Corporation and Non-Profit Housing. Casino and gaming reserve of $4,757,218. Waubetek Business Development Corporation mortgage outstanding.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 but audit report is dated March 5, 2024 — significant delay in audit completion. Two entities fully consolidated: Serpent River First Nation Economic Development Corporation and Serpent River First Nation Non-Profit Housing. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $7,049,493 + Province of Ontario $1,320,538 + Government of Canada $484,408 + OFNLP $568,103 + North Shore Tribal Council $318,161 + Health Canada $51,435 (per deferred revenue note) + CMHC $432,973 (per deferred revenue note) minus overlap adjustments; used statement of operations lines directly: ISC + Province of Ontario + Government of Canada + OFNLP + North Shore Tribal Council = $9,740,703 plus Government of Canada Trust Funds $2,001 = $9,742,704. OSR = total revenue $19,399,591 minus government transfers $9,742,703 = $9,656,888. Current liabilities = accounts payable $1,214,752 + due to funders $507,121 = $1,721,873. No schedule of remuneration provided. Auditor's report contains extraneous text appearing to be from a different engagement letter (Whitefish River First Nation CMHC project), suggesting a document preparation error.
